title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Capstone Defense · Docker Retrieval
summary: An operations reviewer reconstructs daemon operations, upgrades, recovery, cleanup, orchestration boundary, and capstone defense from delayed evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and extends the production Docker operations and release defense under a changed constraint. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for production Docker operations and release defense after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
Plan Engine, API, containerd, runtime, BuildKit, Buildx, Compose, plugin, storage, and deprecated-feature compatibility across staged upgrade and rollback.
Attribute disk use to images, build cache, writable layers, logs, volumes, and snapshots; preserve owners and backups before scoped cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Docker Daemon Config Live Restore · changed case · Review Docker Daemon Config Live Restore
steps.instruction:
review case — A review board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
